name: Shippo API
what_it_does: Multi-carrier shipping API for creating discounted labels, comparing rates, tracking packages, and managing returns across major carriers (USPS, UPS, FedEx, DHL, etc.).
use_cases: Creating shipping labels with pre-negotiated discounted rates
Comparing shipping rates across carriers for e-commerce fulfillment
Automated return label generation for customer returns
Package tracking aggregation across multiple carriers
Manifest creation for end-of-day carrier pickups
not_for: Very high-volume operations where direct carrier contracts are more cost-effective
best_when: An agent needs to create shipping labels with competitive rates for e-commerce orders, especially for small-to-medium businesses that benefit from Shippo's volume-negotiated carrier rates.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-scale shipping management with advanced WMS integration.

auth.notes: Shippo token passed as 'ShippoToken <key>' in Authorization header (not standard Bearer). Test and live tokens are separate. Simple key-based auth with no OAuth complexity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for development; 30 label purchases/month free on Starter
pricing.notes: Starter plan allows limited monthly labels. Postage costs are charged separately at discounted carrier rates. Professional plan removes monthly label limits and adds features like batch labels.

performance.rate_limits: No hard rate limit documented; practical limits exist for fair use

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include 'messages' array with text descriptions. Validation errors show field-level details. Carrier-specific errors are translated to Shippo format. Generally clear and actionable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Metadata field can be used to tag shipments for deduplication but no formal idempotency keys. Agents should track created shipment IDs to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Authorization header format is 'ShippoToken <key>' not 'Bearer <key>' — common mistake
Rate creation and purchase are separate steps — rates expire after a period
Test mode uses USPS simulated labels — not all carriers available in test mode
Address validation is a separate API call and should precede label creation
Async label generation (for some carriers) requires polling status endpoint
International shipments require customs declarations — agents must collect line-item details

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. PII in shipping addresses — handle with care. SOC2 not documented. Test tokens for sandbox. Carrier credentials managed by Shippo.
